4.note: Or to those who took us captive.

6.note: Or the prophets respond; they prophesy.

7.anchor: Should you talk that way, O family of Israel?

5.anchor: Because of the rebellion of Israel

10.anchor: Don’t tell our enemies in Gath
10.note: sounds like the Hebrew term for “tell.” Gath

11.note: sounds like the Hebrew term for “come out.” Zaanan

12.note: sounds like the Hebrew term for “bitter.” Maroth

13.note: sounds like the Hebrew term for “team of horses.” Lachish

14.note: sounds like the Hebrew term for “gift” or “dowry.” Moresheth

15.note: sounds like the Hebrew term for “conqueror.” Mareshah

5.anchor: And remember your journey from Acacia Grove

9.note: Hebrew “Listen to the rod. / Who appointed it?”

10.anchor: of measuring out grain with dishonest measures?
10.note: Hebrew of using the short ephah?

7.anchor: Then the remnant left in Israel

8.note: Hebrew As for you, Migdal-eder, / the Ophel of the daughter of Zion.

11.anchor: “Let us see the destruction of Jerusalem.

13.anchor: “Rise up and crush the nations, O Jerusalem!”
13.note: Hebrew “Rise up and thresh, O daughter of Zion.”

12.anchor: from Egypt all the way to the Euphrates River,

14.anchor: on the heights of Mount Carmel,
14.note: Or surrounded by a fruitful land.
